title: The Microsoft AI Stack for .NET
description: A living presentation powered by AI agents, real-time polls, and the full Microsoft AI stack for .NET.

topics.title: Microsoft.Extensions.AI
topics.description: The abstraction layer everything speaks through — IChatClient, IEmbeddingGenerator, ChatClientBuilder, AIFunctionFactory
talkingPoints: IChatClient — provider-agnostic LLM calls
IEmbeddingGenerator — embeddings for search and ingestion
ChatClientBuilder — composable middleware pipeline
AIFunctionFactory.Create() — tool definitions for agents
One interface, any provider, middleware for cross-cutting concerns

topics.title: Knowledge Engineering
topics.description: DataIngestion pipelines and VectorData stores — turning raw content into searchable, semantic knowledge
talkingPoints: IngestionPipeline — 5-stage document processing
Readers, Chunkers, and Enrichers
InMemoryVectorStore — zero-infrastructure vector search
VectorStoreCollection with auto-embedding
The living knowledge base that grows during the session

suggestedPolls.question: What's the hardest part of building RAG applications?

topics.title: Agentic AI
topics.description: The Microsoft Agent Framework — ChatClientAgent, tools, and AgentWorkflowBuilder for multi-agent orchestration
talkingPoints: ChatClientAgent — agents with tools and instructions
AgentWorkflowBuilder — sequential, handoff, group chat
Tool calling via AIFunctionFactory integration
Multi-agent collaboration patterns
The snowball effect — agents get smarter as the KB grows

topics.title: Interoperability with MCP
topics.description: Model Context Protocol — exposing tools as an MCP server and consuming external knowledge as an MCP client
talkingPoints: MCP server — expose your app's capabilities to any AI client
MCP client — consume external tools and knowledge
Streamable HTTP transport for web scenarios
Tool discovery and invocation protocol
The ecosystem effect — composable AI capabilities
